_note: Semantic (vector-favored) code queries. Authored AND oracle-verified by a Fable-5 subagent against axon src/axon: every relpath in `relevant` was read-checked to exist and be relevant. Merged with auto-generated structural queries by scripts/build_code_query_set.py.

queries.question: How does AXON decide that an old decision has been superseded in the recall ranking, and what penalty does it receive?
queries.answer_key: Soft supersession (dec-115): same scope + an EN/PT revision verb in the newer summary OR near-duplicate cosine >= 0.93; rank x 0.02 (demote, never delete).

queries.question: How is the cache key for the prompt context layers built, and what components go into it?
queries.answer_key: build_composite_cache_key joins sha256(content) | ctx | policy_version | model | provider-availability; the router caches static/semi-static layers with it.

queries.question: How does the chunker split source code into chunks for indexing — which units become chunks and what is the size limit?
queries.answer_key: Tree-sitter (Java/Python/TS/TSX) extracts methods, constructors, classes, interfaces, enums, records, and annotations; _MAX_CHUNK_LINES = 80.

queries.question: Which LLM models do the FREE and PAID provider profiles map each task type to?
queries.answer_key: FREE -> Groq llama-3.1-8b/3.3-70b + NVIDIA NIM llama-3.1-70b per tier; PAID -> OpenRouter Claude haiku-4/sonnet-4/opus-4; classifier groq/llama-3.1-8b-instant.

queries.question: What happens when a SQLite write fails with SQLITE_BUSY — how do the retry and fallback work?
queries.answer_key: with_retry: exponential backoff with jitter within a budget (10s); once exhausted, it serializes to .axon/pending/ via atomic rename and replays via drain, with quarantine (dec-112).

queries.question: How does the ADR density gate decide a rationale is genuine commentary rather than boilerplate or a paraphrase of the diff?
queries.answer_key: Requires >=1 architectural-lexicon token absent from the diff and rejects when >85% of rationale tokens overlap the diff (checks relaxed in structural mode).

queries.question: Where and how does AXON block destructive MCP tools without explicit user consent?
queries.answer_key: PolicyEngine.decide_tool_action denies risk=destructive (DENY_DESTRUCTIVE_NO_CONSENT) without AXON_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE, and write/destructive in a restricted ctx; @traced_tool re-checks on every call.
